headline: SVP's 10-million population cap polling at 52% with three weeks to June 14 vote.
summary: The latest SRG/SSR poll (late April 2026) shows 52% of respondents backing the SVP initiative "No to a Switzerland of 10 million", up from near-even splits in December.

headline: National Council committee backs constitutional anchor for Bilaterals III.
summary: A parliamentary committee stated on 22 May 2026 that the Bilaterals III package must be embedded in the Federal Constitution via a transitional article, aligning with the Council of States committee's earlier position.

headline: NCSC National Cyberstrategy progress report: 325 mandatory incident reports logged, AI risks addressed.
summary: The Swiss National Cyber Security Centre published its latest NCS implementation report on 20 May 2026, documenting that Switzerland's mandatory critical-infrastructure reporting regime (in force since April 2025) has generated 325 formal notifications — 25% from public administration, 18% IT/teleco

headline: Switzerland now "prime target" for cybercriminals, NCSC data show.
summary: Cybersecurity experts quoted in *The Local* (20 May 2026) cite Switzerland's prominence in finance, pharma, and electronics as making it a high-value target.

headline: Leuk Starlink antenna project faces petition with 14,550 signatures.
summary: Opponents submitted a petition in Bern on 22 May 2026 demanding the Federal Council halt plans to install 40 Starlink satellite antennas in Leuk, canton Valais.

headline: China's deadliest mine disaster in over a decade: 90 killed in Shanxi gas explosion.
summary: A methane explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Changzhi, Shanxi province struck on Friday evening when approximately 247 workers were underground.

headline: Ebola PHEIC declared: Bundibugyo strain in DRC and Uganda, no approved vaccine.
summary: On 17 May 2026, WHO Director-General Tedros declared the DRC/Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern — only the seventh such declaration in WHO history.

headline: Rubio in India: repair mode for US–India ties after China summit and India–Pakistan ceasefire.
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on 22 May for a three-day visit encompassing Kolkata, Agra, Jaipur, and New Delhi.

headline: Bolivia: capital under siege as protests kill 4, drain $50 million daily.
summary: Two weeks of roadblocks led by the Bolivian Workers' Central (COB), peasant unions, and miners have emptied La Paz markets, depleted hospital oxygen reserves, and stranded about 5,000 vehicles on highways.

headline: Russia–Ukraine: slow territorial attrition, peace talks "on pause".
summary: Russia suffered a net loss of 69 square miles of Ukrainian territory in the April 21 – May 19 period.

headline: US–Iran nuclear MOU: enrichment moratorium duration the last sticking point.
summary: Axios reported on 6 May that the two sides were "closing in" on a 14-point memorandum of understanding.

headline: Laravel-Lang PHP supply chain attack: 700+ versions backdoored with RCE credential stealer.
summary: On 22 May 2026, a threat actor with push access to the Laravel-Lang GitHub organisation rewrote every git tag across four popular Composer packages within a 15-minute window, pointing legitimate-looking release tags to commits inside a malicious fork: `laravel-lang/lang` (7.8k stars), `/attributes`,

headline: CVE-2026-48172 (CVSS 10.0) — LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin: root code execution, actively exploited.
summary: An incorrect privilege-assignment flaw in LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin versions 2.3–2.4.4 allows any cPanel user (or attacker with cPanel credentials) to invoke `lsws.redisAble` and execute arbitrary scripts as root.

headline: CVE-2026-20223 (CVSS 10.0) — Cisco Secure Workload: unauthenticated API access.
summary: Insufficient validation and authentication checks in internal REST API endpoints of Cisco Secure Workload allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to read sensitive data and make arbitrary configuration changes.

headline: CVE-2026-41091 & CVE-2026-45498 — Microsoft Defender zero-days added to CISA KEV; federal deadline June 3.
summary: CISA added both flaws to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on 20 May 2026.

headline: NGINX Rift — CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS v4: 9.2): 18-year-old heap overflow under active exploitation since May 16.
summary: Disclosed 13 May by F5 and depthfirst, this heap buffer overflow in `ngx_http_rewrite_module` (present in all NGINX builds 0.6.27–1.30.0 and NGINX Plus R32–R36) stems from a two-pass contract violation: an `is_args` flag set during the length-calculation phase leaks into the copy phase, causing `ngx

headline: Chinese APT SHADOW-EARTH-053 targets Asian governments, one NATO state, journalists, and activists.
summary: Researchers disclosed details in May 2026 of a Chinese-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defence sectors in Pakistan, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, and Taiwan, plus Poland (the lone NATO state in the victimology).
